# Ep. 15: The Record-Keeper, with Epic’s Judy Faulkner

SHARE

* [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://milkeninstitute.org/content-hub/podcasts/ep-15-record-keeper-epics-judy-faulkner&title=Ep. 15: The Record-Keeper, with Epic’s Judy Faulkner&source=https://milkeninstitute.org/content-hub/podcasts/ep-15-record-keeper-epics-judy-faulkner "Linkedin")
* [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://milkeninstitute.org/content-hub/podcasts/ep-15-record-keeper-epics-judy-faulkner&url=https://milkeninstitute.org/content-hub/podcasts/ep-15-record-keeper-epics-judy-faulkner&hashtags=Hashtag "Twitter")
* [](https://www.facebook.com/share.php?u=https://milkeninstitute.org/content-hub/podcasts/ep-15-record-keeper-epics-judy-faulkner&title=Ep. 15: The Record-Keeper, with Epic’s Judy Faulkner "Facebook")

Audio file

**“We do have a culture of ownership, of working hard, of wanting to be heroes helping heroes. That's one of the things you hear our staff say.”**

The story is familiar, even mythic: brilliant young student builds out a new technology in her garage and changes the world. But Judy Faulkner never made it to Silicon Valley. The medical software company she founded in a Madison basement four decades ago remains in Wisconsin—while Epic’s importance to the world of health care continues to grow.

More than 250 million patient medical records are on the Epic platform, and that data could yield valuable clues in the search for solutions to the pandemic. It’s all part of the employee-owned company’s mission to support the heroes on the front lines.
